title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 25th February 2026 || TV5 News

recommendations.note: Not a bad stock; brands; recovers when retailing improves
recommendations.detail: Hold all three (ABFRL, MSUMI, Adani Power). ABFRL is not a bad stock with a lot of brands; when retailing improves again it will automatically jump up. A Kumar Mangalam Birla group company on a weak wicket post-demerger, but recovery at some point along with economic growth could come.
Among the three stocks the caller held (ABFRL, MSUMI, Adani Power), Kutumba Rao advised holding all three. The caller was at breakeven on Motherson Sumi Wiring.

recommendations.note: Hold; no need to be scared
recommendations.detail: Hold Adani Power; no need to be scared much. He added that power companies have good times due to data-center demand, and viewed Trump's rate-payer protection pledge for AI data centers as a positive for the space globally.
Avoid as of now. Even at current levels he can't rule out more downside. The stock already tested 250 and rebounded to 330-340, but failed to cross there and fell again.

recommendations.note: Buy in the offer-for-sale at the lowest point
recommendations.detail: Buy in the offer-for-sale opening today at Rs 104 (5% discount to market), as it's the lowest point. Value is around Rs 100, and at ~20 PE it becomes an attractive valuation. It is a simple, steady finance company with a moat, although it was earlier over-hyped (rose from Rs 22-25 to 150-160 in the railway-stock frenzy).

recommendations.note: Buy for long term only; value after correction
recommendations.detail: Buy for the long term only, not short term. After the recent correction value appears. It is especially in an exciting industry given post-climate-change water requirements and desalination demand.

recommendations.note: Buy for long term only; value after correction
recommendations.detail: Buy for the long term only, not short term. After the recent correction value appears in the stock.

recommendations.note: Avoid; exit on rallies, don't buy
recommendations.detail: Unnecessary stock; exit on rallies. It is a perennial 'turnaround' story that pops Rs 40-50 then gets hammered below par. As of now at the bottom he says avoid, and not to buy even on rallies.

recommendations.note: Weak stock; not worth even at this price
recommendations.detail: A weak stock he has repeatedly flagged. A COVID-era hype name that has made lower tops and lower bottoms since listing and hasn't risen; near 40 PE and near its 52-week low. Results came stable recently but it is definitely not worth even at this price.

recommendations.note: Hold long term; attractive valuation near 52-week low
recommendations.detail: Hold for the long term as of now. Near its 52-week low; setting aside the Shapoorji Pallonji-group overhang it is one of the larger infra players with a good order book at an attractive valuation. The majority of the Shapoorji stake is pledged; if the group gains liquidity (e.g., from a Tata Sons exit) the stock will improve.
He has called it avoidable from the delisting/relisting onwards and still says avoidable now; exit on rallies. He also told a separate caller to hold Wipro rather than switch into Hexaware.
Hold HDFC Life (referred to as HFC Life). The caller was at 28% profit; he advised holding rather than booking, in contrast to South Indian Bank where he advised booking profits.
Definitely book profits in South Indian Bank (caller at 29% profit). He contrasted this with HDFC Life, which he advised holding.

recommendations.note: Hold; can reinvent itself, large cash reserves
recommendations.detail: Hold Wipro instead of switching to Hexaware. Wipro has the capability of reinventing itself and has large cash reserves, which is its biggest advantage.
